nodes.content: Snowpack finished far below normal and soil moisture is already depleted across the basin. Reservoir operators say carryover storage is weaker than last year.
nodes.label: climate_risk signal T0

classification.rationale: The signal describes depleted water resources with below-normal snowpack and soil moisture, indicating a significant but not extreme water shortage.

nodes.content: The basin faces significant water scarcity entering the season, with depleted snowpack and soil moisture combined with weaker reservoir carryover storage than last year. This creates a challenging starting point for water management during the upcoming dry period.
nodes.label: climate_risk inference T0

key_mechanisms: Reduced snowpack limits spring/summer meltwater inflows to reservoirs
Low soil moisture increases irrigation demand and reduces natural water retention
Weaker reservoir storage reduces buffer capacity for drought conditions
Multiple water deficits compound to create systemic water stress

violations.description: Null-policy rollout triggered a hard stop

nodes.content: A severe early heatwave arrived, emergency water restrictions were delayed, and orchard stress is rising as reservoir levels fall toward critical thresholds.
nodes.label: climate_risk signal T1

classification.rationale: The signal describes a heatwave, water restrictions, and falling reservoir levels indicating a climate-related drought event.

nodes.content: A severe early heatwave has intensified drought conditions, with delayed emergency water restrictions exacerbating water scarcity as reservoir levels approach critical thresholds and orchard stress rises, compounding existing deficits from depleted snowpack and soil moisture.
nodes.label: climate_risk inference T1

key_mechanisms: Heatwave increases evaporation and water demand, accelerating reservoir depletion and crop stress.
Delayed policy response reduces adaptive capacity, allowing water shortages to worsen unchecked.
Falling reservoir levels toward critical thresholds heighten risk of supply failures and agricultural losses.
Compounding effects from prior water deficits create a feedback loop of escalating stress.
